page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the n4d server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from neighboring virtual machines. With 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, this instance type is structured to handle memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per core allows organizations to run memory-bound applications without paying for unnecessary additional vCPUs.

This server type is suitable for workloads such as small-to-medium databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise applications, and lightweight data processing pipelines that require a larger memory footprint relative to compute capacity.

This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it suitable for memory-intensive operations. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable performance for workloads requiring consistent processing power. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-effective resource profile for applications that demand significant memory capacity without the need for high compute core counts. It is well-suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
